description_long: This is a medium-sized, timber-framed cabin with a prominent gable roof and a large, rectangular chimney rising along one side. The exterior features a combination of dark brown wooden beams and white infill panels, giving it a Tudor-inspired appearance. Multiple windows with vertical divisions are symmetrically arranged on the front and side facades, allowing ample natural light inside. The roof is steeply pitched with intersecting gables, and the overhangs provide some shelter to the entryways and windows. A small covered entryway is visible on one side, suggesting a main entrance, while the overall structure is elevated slightly above ground on short supports. The cabin's design is both functional and decorative, suitable for residential use in an urban or suburban neighborhood.
description: A timber-framed, Tudor-style cabin with a steep gable roof, large chimney, and multiple symmetrically arranged windows. The exterior features dark brown beams, white panels, and a small covered entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with large windows, the prominent chimney, and the covered entryway.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the intersecting gable roofs and the side extension of the cabin.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the symmetrical window arrangement and the full width of the main roof.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the chimney, side structure, and additional windows.
attribute_roof_type: steep intersecting gable roof with overhangs, constructed from dark wooden shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple medium-sized rectangular windows with vertical divisions, symmetrically arranged on the front and side facades, forming a balanced and repetitive pattern
attribute_door_placement: Single main door located on the front facade beneath the covered entryway, slightly offset to one side
